records.subject: UC-01
facts: Fresh task uc01_direct_forward answered the Git rebase request in one sentence with no delegation or writes.
Only the packaged skill contract was read to load the requested behavior.

records.subject: UC-03
facts: Fresh task uc03_diagnose_forward proved that canonical .agents guidance and generated Codex/Claude projections can diverge between apply and doctor checks.
The task remained read-only, wrote no files, and reported the available PowerShell doctor PASSED with exit code 0.

records.subject: UC-04
facts: Fresh gpt-5.6-sol task uc04_audit_forward remained read-only and reported evidence-backed findings with file and line references.
It found no HIGH issue, identified control-file reparse and rule-path portability gaps as MEDIUM, and those findings were covered by new Python and PowerShell corpus cases before release.

records.subject: UC-19
facts: Fresh task uc19_fanout_forward derived U-001 through U-037 from the 37 enumerated fixture units, one synthesis job, and conditional verifier jobs.
It separated the logical total 38+F from four live instances including the lead, yielding three worker slots and 13 base waves without dropping coverage.

records.subject: UC-23
facts: Fresh regression task reported four logical tickets separately from one live agent and explained snapshot freshness in the user's language.
It reported requested and effective model and effort per ticket, explained Terra low, Sol xhigh, inheritance, fallback, and unobservable runtime values without writes or tests.

records.subject: UC-24
facts: A fresh gpt-5.6-sol verifier ran two init plans on a disposable CRLF AGENTS.md fixture; both exited 0 with identical plan ID, identical stdout hash, and unchanged byte-plus-mtime snapshots.
The five writes matched changes one-for-one; every exact content value produced its declared SHA-256 and the applied bytes matched those writes.
Apply touched only config, manifest, context, Claude root, and the AGENTS managed block; the original 141-byte CRLF prefix remained byte-identical.
The second plan and apply both returned NO_CHANGES with empty writes and unchanged bytes and mtimes; the Python doctor returned PASSED with exit code 0.

records.subject: UC-25
facts: A fresh gpt-5.6-sol verifier initialized a rich disposable fixture and obtained an edit plan with exactly five dependent updates.
After the planned Codex projection was changed, apply returned CONFLICT with exit code 2 and a recursive hash-size-mtime snapshot proved zero configurator writes.

After explicit restoration and adoption into the canonical reviewer, the new confirmed plan changed only config, manifest, that reviewer, and its Codex and Claude projections; two forward-compatible keys survived and the final Python doctor returned PASSED exit 0.

records.subject: UC-29
facts: Fresh task uc29_controls_forward resolved the current confirm-before-write, phase-and-barrier, standard-detail, visible job/file/check, conversation-only, auto-concurrency, and safety-cap 1000 settings.
It derived eight unit jobs plus one synthesis across three worker waves and kept the task read-only.
The current policy now keeps the lead inherited, requests Terra for bounded jobs and Sol for verifiers, and reports inherit fallback only when runtime evidence requires it.

records.subject: UC-30
facts: The live Python and PowerShell harnesses both returned MATCH for empty-manifest and omitted-config, with doctor status FAILED and exit code 1.
Diagnostics identified the incomplete manifest and the omitted required config entry.

Both disposable 44-case corpora completed with 44 matches, zero mismatches, and zero NOT_AVAILABLE cases, including reasoning policy, exact model-ID acceptance, rule-path portability, control-file reparse rejection, routing, casing, canonical skills, adapters, and blocks.

records.subject: UC-31
facts: Both live 44-case harnesses created managed-path and control-directory reparse fixtures and the doctors returned FAILED with exit code 1.

Both rejected .ultracode/config.json, .ultracode/managed.json, and managed paths before reading or hashing linked targets; the configurator 8/8 corpus additionally rejected nested and top-level Claude root candidates before selection, decoding, or hashing.

records.subject: UC-32
facts: Both live harnesses rejected semantic rule and skill adapters with missing or contrary canonical directives, despite refreshed managed hashes.
Both also rejected incorrect adapter markers and ambiguous role projections rather than trusting byte hashes alone.

records.subject: UC-34
facts: Python and PowerShell doctor corpora each completed 44/44 matches and rejected malformed reasoning policy, invalid effort ordering, Plan_Gate, upper-case artifact or role identifiers, non-portable rule-path selectors, and a newline-terminated model ID.
The contract casing corpus is recorded separately as a release validation.

records.subject: UC-35
facts: Fresh regression task used the packaged 0.5 flow snapshot fixture and remained read-only.

It explained active, blocked, queued, and completed tickets with real U-* IDs, responsibility, live agent, requested and effective model and effort, routing reason or inheritance, remaining work, blocker, completion criterion, and next action in Italian.
It invented no fallback, percentage, ETA, agent, model, effort, or runtime state.

records.subject: UC-36
facts: Fresh task uc36_bundled_python_forward preserved the original source-change objective and detected the missing config and managed manifest before project writes.
Using the Codex Desktop bundled Python runtime, it generated deterministic init plan 0b41e1109ff02c4f894f6aa98dec23cbcd5891cb866e017b786c1a11458bec86 with a doctor-valid proposed state.
It explained the conservative baseline, preserved existing AGENTS.md content, requested one confirmation, promised to resume the original task after apply, and changed no project files.

records.subject: UC-37
facts: Fresh 0.5.2 bare and brief Terra low contexts rendered the required chat hierarchy and semantic content read-only.
A raw `$ultracode-help flow` context selected Flow and reconstructed live state; the source contract was repaired so every non-Help skill defers an explicit Help invocation before action.
A new fresh source context returned only focused Flow help with four labeled fields, an inline blockquote example, and the nearest comparison; it did not inspect or invent live state.

After the H1 gate changed the prompt text, a final fresh context received the exact current default prompt and produced exactly one H1, six ordered H2 areas, six H3 command sections, quick-choice/model/ticket tables, four fields and one blockquote per command, preflight behavior, and authority boundaries.
No successful Help evaluation initialized, delegated, ran project checks, changed global settings, or wrote files.

records.subject: UC-38
facts: Fresh Terra low task independently routed the four fixture jobs to Terra low, Terra high, Sol xhigh, and independent Sol xhigh from their ambiguity, coupling, evidence, and consequence.

It distinguished Sol medium pre-task startup guidance from the inherited active lead, changed no global setting, separated requested from effective model and effort, explained context and fallback constraints, and executed no work.

records.subject: reparse boundary fixture Python

records.subject: reparse boundary fixture PowerShell

records.subject: quick_validate ultracode
facts: Official skill-creator validator returned Skill is valid after the control, doctor, and payload-gate edits.

records.subject: quick_validate ultracode-help
facts: Official skill-creator validator returned Skill is valid for the chat-friendly Help layout and explicit command-precedence contract.

records.subject: quick_validate ultracode-init
facts: Official skill-creator validator returned Skill is valid after the control, doctor, and payload-gate edits.

records.subject: quick_validate ultracode-edit
facts: Official skill-creator validator returned Skill is valid after the control, doctor, and payload-gate edits.

records.subject: quick_validate ultracode-flow
facts: Official skill-creator validator returned Skill is valid for the new read-only flow skill.

records.subject: quick_validate ultracode-status
facts: Official skill-creator validator returned Skill is valid after the control, doctor, and payload-gate edits.

records.subject: validate_plugin
facts: Official plugin-creator validator returned Plugin validation passed after the control, doctor, and payload-gate edits.

records.subject: contract checker Python bootstrap
facts: Python accepted the 0.5.2 chat layout, per-command structure, table headers, inline blockquotes, explicit Help precedence in all five non-Help skills, semantic routing, prompt values, skill hashes, payload hash, and trace bindings.
It completed the live doctor corpus and returned exit 0 with only the independent audit pending.

records.subject: contract checker PowerShell bootstrap
facts: PowerShell independently enforced the same 0.5.2 layout, precedence, semantic, prompt-value, hash, and trace gates.
It completed its live doctor corpus and returned exit 0 with only the independent audit pending.

records.subject: contract casing corpus
facts: The expanded disposable-copy corpus completed 34/34 matches with zero mismatches and zero unavailable runtimes.

Python and PowerShell passed the baseline and rejected evidence casing, schema expansion, a demoted H1, missing command fields, missing inline prompt tokens, broken quick/model/ticket tables, flattened blockquotes, removed Flow Help precedence, routing semantics, Init preflight, and weakened metadata or manifest prompt values.
The metadata mutation kept all required phrases only in a YAML comment while reducing the actual default_prompt to a generic invocation; both validators still rejected it.

records.subject: project configurator corpus
facts: The disposable corpus completed 9/9 cases with exit code 0.

It proved a project below a linked ancestor remains valid after canonical root normalization, while invalid config, non-portable selectors, control, managed, nested-Claude, and top-level-Claude reparse traversal, unmanaged markers, collisions, and drift are rejected before selection, read, hash, or project writes.
A forced Windows mid-apply PermissionError restored every earlier file and left the doctor-valid project byte-identical.
It also proved deterministic plans, confirmed apply, no-op behavior, rich adapters, unknown-key preservation, and localized edits.

records.subject: final independent high-severity audit
facts: Independent Sol xhigh review found one material evidence gap: UC-37 required one H1, but the three validators and adversarial corpus did not enforce it.
The repair added a canonical one-H1 gate to the repository, Python, and PowerShell validators, changed both default prompts to request one H1 explicitly, and added paired adversarial H1 cases.

Re-audit verified repository validation exit 0, Python and PowerShell bootstrap checks exit 0, Python AST and PowerShell parser success, both project doctors exit 0, git diff check exit 0, 34/34 adversarial cases, exact current default-prompt behavior, explicit Help precedence, 6/6 skill hashes, payload and trace bindings, and 38 result-to-trace bindings.
No HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW findings remain.
